methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 10 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Atomic Semi is a Seed-stage semiconductor company founded by Sam Zeloof and Jim Keller that has raised $15M+. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 11 peers in the Chips & Semiconductors sector: 3 public companies (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private firms including Cerebras, SambaNova Systems, PsiQuantum, Etched, and Tenstorrent.

The stage-progression method is the applicable triangulation technique here. Using total capital raised of $15M as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $38M. This reflects the expected step-up in valuation upon progression beyond seed financing.

The peer-median approach is not applicable due to insufficient narrow-set comparables. While 10 same-sector peers exist, none yielded three or more datapoints when filtered by same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier, making sector-wide median comparison potentially misleading. The analysis therefore relies on stage-progression alone. The triangulated range is $32M (low), $38M (median), and $43M (high), centered on the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the actual burn rate and path to Series A closing, competitive positioning within the semiconductor IP and manufacturing space, and whether the 2.5x multiplier holds given the nascent, capital-intensive nature of frontier chip development.
